compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## What

Generate `docs/mcp-reference.md` from the MCP server's OWN registrars, correctly
classified by gate, and gate it in CI. Mirrors the `gen_env_registry.py` generator +
drift-gate pattern.

## Source of truth (verified ŌĆö registration-time vs call-time gating)
The server (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py` `build_server()`) assembles THREE registrars:
- `register_read_tools` (`_mcp_reads.py`) ŌĆö 23 tools, ALWAYS registered.
- `register_llm_tools` (`_mcp_llm.py`) ŌĆö 6 tools, ALWAYS registered; gated at CALL time on
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`.
- `register_write_tools` (`_mcp_writes.py`) ŌĆö 18 tools, CONDITIONALLY registered: returns
  early when `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` is truthy, so they are ABSENT from `list_tools()` in
  read-only mode. Totals verified: 47 tools (readonly off) / 29 (readonly on).

**Critical:** only `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` changes list membership. `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` and
`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` are call-time only (tools always listed, refuse at call). So a
naive single `list_tools()` in ambient env silently produces a PARTIAL reference. The
generator MUST enumerate each registrar with `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0` so writes register.

## Classification rule (deterministic ŌĆö registrar default + closed 3-entry special-case table)
There is NO per-tool gate attribute, and `readOnlyHint`/`destructiveHint` do NOT map to the
env gate. Classify by which registrar registers the tool, then apply the closed override table:
| Group (registrar) | Default gate | Special-case overrides |
|---|---|---|
| READS | none (always available) | `reconcile` -> mutating modes blocked by `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` FIRST, then require `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` (BOTH gate live mode; dry-run always works); `fsck` -> recover path gated by `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` |
| LLM | `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` (call-time) | `sign_review` -> `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` (HYBRID: in the LLM registrar but write-gated, makes no LLM call) |
| WRITES | `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` (registration-time; absent when readonly) | `run_workflow` -> write-gated by `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` AND, for a non-dry-run workflow that HAS LLM steps, additionally gated at call time by `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` (a second hybrid) |

This special-case table is CLOSED at FOUR entries (`reconcile`, `fsck`, `sign_review`,
`run_workflow`); the generator carries them explicitly and a drift check asserts these four
names still exist in their expected registrars, so a new hybrid is caught rather than
silently misclassified.
`sign_manifest` is a WRITE-registrar tool (not the LLM `sign_review` ŌĆö do not conflate).
Gate descriptions come from the `MCP_ENV_VARS` constant (`mcp_server.py`), the same source
`rebar-mcp --help` uses, so the doc cannot drift from `--help`.

## Scope
- Add `scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py` (new): enumerate each registrar onto its own FastMCP
  with `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=0` (writes register); read each tool's name + first-line summary
  (from `.description`); classify via the table above; emit `docs/mcp-reference.md` with a
  generated header and exactly THREE clearly-labeled gate-tier sections:
  **Read-only (always)**, **LLM-gated (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`)**, and
  **Write-gated (`REBAR_MCP_READONLY`)**. The three special cases are inline ANNOTATIONS,
  NOT separate sections: `reconcile` under Read-only annotated "live/mutating modes blocked by
  `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` and require `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`"; `fsck` under Read-only annotated "recover path gated by
  `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`"; `sign_review` under Write-gated (NOT LLM-gated) annotated "hybrid: in the LLM registrar,
  write-gated"; `run_workflow` under Write-gated annotated "live LLM-step workflows also
  require `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`". Include the `MCP_ENV_VARS` gate descriptions.
- `--check` drift mode + CI gate in BOTH `.github/workflows/test.yml` AND
  `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (the actual Verified gate; a parity test
  `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` enforces every `scripts/*.py` in test.yml also
  appears in gerrit-verify.yaml ŌĆö omitting it breaks `make test`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/mcp-reference.md` is generated (marked generated) from the live registrars and
      lists all 47 tools (23 read + 6 llm + 18 write).
- [ ] Tools are structurally separated into labeled sections by gate tier (NOT prose) ŌĆö a
      Read-only section, an LLM-gated section, and a Write-gated section each exist (verify,
      prints nothing):
      `for s in 'Read-only' 'LLM-gated' 'Write-gated'; do grep -qi "$s" docs/mcp-reference.md || echo "NO SECTION $s"; done`
- [ ] The three special cases are correctly placed ŌĆö DETERMINISTIC section-bounded proof
      (all three sub-checks print nothing). `sign_review` is in the Write-gated section and
      NOT in the LLM-gated section; `reconcile` is annotated jira-gated; `fsck` annotated
      readonly-recover:
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py`
      then a held-out unit test asserts, using section-bounded slicing of
      `docs/mcp-reference.md` (awk from a tier heading to the next `## `): `sign_review` row
      is inside the Write-gated section and absent from the LLM-gated section; the `reconcile`
      row text contains BOTH `REBAR_MCP_READONLY` and `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`; the
      `run_workflow` row (Write-gated) text contains `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`; the `fsck` row text contains
      `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`. (The section-bounded assertion is the independent oracle ŌĆö it
      does not just re-run the generator's own classifier.)
- [ ] Count proof (deterministic, prints nothing):
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py && [ "$(grep -cE '^\| ?`?[a-z_]+`? ' docs/mcp-reference.md)" -ge 47 ] || echo "TOO FEW TOOLS"` (or an equivalent explicit count of listed tool rows >= 47).
- [ ] Drift gate works ŌĆö adding/removing a tool changes output; a stale committed file fails
      `--check` (deterministic, prints `CAUGHT`):
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py; printf '\nstale\n' >> docs/mcp-reference.md; python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py --check >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MISS || echo CAUGHT; python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py`
- [ ] The gate is wired in BOTH CI files (prints nothing):
      `for f in .github/workflows/test.yml .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml; do grep -qF 'gen_mcp_reference.py' "$f" || echo "NOT WIRED: $f"; done`
      and `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py -q` passes.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` stay green.

Files: `scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py` (new), `docs/mcp-reference.md` (new/generated),
`tests/unit/test_gen_mcp_reference.py` (new ŌĆö the held-out section-bounded placement +
enumeration tests, including the AC3 special-case placement assertions),
`.github/workflows/test.yml`, `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`.
compiled_state.title: P1: Generate the MCP tool reference (generator + drift gate)
